output.summary: Enable flex-wrap on the social media icon list so items break to the next line when they exceed the available width, instead of overflowing. Remove shrink-0 from the nav element so the flex container can occupy available space and allow wrapping. The md:flex-wrap class already exists on the <ul> but shrink-0 on the parent <nav> prevents the container from shrinking, which can cause overflow instead of wrapping.

fileChanges.description: Remove shrink-0 from nav className to allow the social links container to shrink and wrap properly
fileChanges.details: In the <nav> element, change the className from 'shrink-0 relative md:ml-auto' to 'relative md:ml-auto'. The shrink-0 class prevents the nav from shrinking within the flex parent, which causes icons to overflow instead of the <ul> wrapping its children. The md:flex-wrap on the <ul> is already correct.

fileChanges.description: Remove shrink-0 from nav class to match TSX change
fileChanges.details: In the <nav> element, change class from 'shrink-0 md:ml-auto' to 'md:ml-auto'. This mirrors the TSX change and ensures the frontend nav container allows its child <ul> to wrap icons to a new line when space is constrained.

designDecisions.decision: Remove shrink-0 from nav to allow flex-wrap to work on the icon list
designDecisions.evidence: User requested icons wrap to a new line instead of overflowing. The <ul> already has md:flex-wrap but the parent nav's shrink-0 prevents the container from being narrow enough to trigger wrapping.
